absolute: Philips Series 3000 Airfryer repeatedly shuts off completely — issue confirmed by MediaMarkt, but Philips found no fault
block: Philips Series 3000 Airfryer repeatedly shuts off completely — issue confirmed by MediaMarkt, but Philips found no fault
md: Hi everyone,I’m hoping someone can help me because I’m getting pretty frustrated with my Philips Series 3000 Airfryer.The problem is that the airfryer randomly shuts off completely while I’m using it. It’s not just that the heating stops or that it pauses — the entire device turns off. The display goes completely black, as if it has lost all power.At first, I thought it might be something with the power outlet at home, so I tested it in another outlet. The problem still happened. I also made sure it wasn’t an issue with an extension cable or something similar.Because the problem continued, I brought it back to MediaMarkt, where I bought it. A MediaMarkt employee tested the airfryer in the store himself, and during that test the airfryer also shut off completely. So the problem was not only happening at my home — it was also happening in the store.After that, MediaMarkt sent it to Philips for inspection. Philips checked the airfryer and said they could not find any problem. Because of that, they didn’t repair or replace anything.However, when I got the airfryer back and tested it at home, it still had exactly the same issue. It still randomly turns off completely.The airfryer is around one year old, so it is not an old device. It has been used normally. It is not extremely dirty, and I don’t believe dirt is the cause, because the entire device loses power when it happens.What makes this situation confusing is that the problem has already been confirmed in the store by an employee, but Philips says they cannot find anything wrong with it.I would like to ask:Has anyone else had this problem with a Philips Series 3000 Airfryer?Could this be a power supply issue, overheating protection, or an internal defect?What should I do if Philips says there is no problem, but the airfryer clearly still does not work correctly?Is it reasonable to ask MediaMarkt for a replacement after the first inspection did not solve the issue?I would really appreciate any advice or experiences from people who have had a similar situation.

absolute: Instant Vortex Plus not working
block: Instant Vortex Plus not working
md: My Instant Vortex air fryer intermittently will not start after the door is closed. This problem can be caused by the door actuator located at the top center of the door, not actuating the switch on the air fryer body. My solution was to take a small piece of metal tape (adhesive one side) and form it around the actuator only. You may have to add 2 or  3 layers to get it high enough to actuate the switch. Our Vortex Plus is about 4 or 5 years old and I suspect the actuator has worn down enough to loose proprt contact. You can you metal tape used for duct sealing, available at any hardware store. Hope it helps

md: So i was just sold a new Kirby vacuum. It too a while to remember and recognize the company. Turns out my Mother in law has one and loves hers even after having it for over a decade. However im being told that they are like an MLM and the way they pressure their sells red on commission only is problematic. My experience with the sales reps I had today and the demo I got went so well they had me immediately. I also have a scrappy ryobi vacuum, so it was likely very easy to sell to me. After already being sold and going through the process of buying this vacuum, I asked if they would be cool if I just spread my support for them on my platform as a vtuber. Like some sort of sponsorship. They then said they would give me $100 for every vacuum I sold through my platform which was way more than I was gonna ask for. Honestly I dont really have views and have a small community im still building. Honestly right now just getting my views up is all I really need. Now im being told that I probably shouldn't due to there high pressure methods and how past employees disliked the high pressure and commission only fees. Im conflicted now because I understand the struggles of being a sales reps and retail and I also like the product I got. At the same time if the company is actually corrupt or problematic, i dont want yo support something that could be harmful. I dont feel like I've been scammed or pressured into this, but apparently there are others who have.

absolute: The Australian robot vacuum fire is a reminder about electrical safety — not a reason to panic
block: The Australian robot vacuum fire is a reminder about electrical safety — not a reason to panic
md: I’ve been following the latest news from Australia: According to reports, a 25-year-old man suffered severe burns after a residential fire was caused by a malfunctioning robot vacuum cleaner. First of all, I sincerely hope he makes a full recovery.As far as I know, the exact cause is still under investigation. It could be the battery, the charging system, the internal wiring, or something else entirely, so I think it’s too early to draw any conclusions.What I’ve been thinking about is this: the greatest danger may not be the robot itself “exploding,” but rather what would happen if an electrical malfunction caused a fire that spread to everything around it.I don’t think anyone needs to panic over this, because electrical safety deserves more attention than the safety of the robot vacuum’s battery itself. For example, we should regularly check the battery for signs of swelling, overheating, or strange odors, and make sure to use the original charging equipment, avoiding cheap replacement parts.Has this incident made you think more deeply about how to use robot vacuums safely? Or has it made you more cautious about electrical safety in your home?

absolute: Why are the newer generation Dreames so slow?
block: Why are the newer generation Dreames so slow?
md: I had an old MOVA S10 Plus a few years back, 7,000pa suction, no wash station etc, no camera for obstacle avoidance (so it sucked in that regard) but it cleaned very quickly. Fast forward today with the Dreame L50 Ultra I've had it for half a year. The suction more than doubled to 20,000pa it's a great vacuum and it does its job very well but it's just slow? It moves slowly, vacuums slowly, it's very very cautious. It's obstacle avoidance is fantastic of course it hasn't snagged a cable or pet toy during my time of owning it. But when it comes across an obstacle it'll stop, back up slowly, then slowly move around, turn it's body around to look at it, move back, move forward and repeat. Does it move slowly to avoid getting trapped on obstacles or is it something else? Because even in a room with no obstacles (carpeted) it moves slowly and yes the carpet deep clean, slower movement settings have been turned off. Not trying to complain because it is a fantastic vacuum but does anyone know why it's so slow?

md: Scoring inconsistencies that I've seen have been Vacuum Wars deep carpet cleaning test with sand 90% for the X60, 89% for the Saros 20 but then Vacuum Nerds, who actually get most units from manufacturers for free so I'm skeptical of their results, they rated the scattered sand pickup test on carpets at 65% for sprinkled sand and 61% for ground in sand for the x60. For the Saros 20 it was 96% for scattered sand and 76% for ground in sand. Jaime Andrews had them within a few percentage points of each other in his rice pick up test on carpets. But then you search these types of subreddits and they say how newer Roborocks are awful for carpet cleaning and that the duo divide is mostly only great at preventing hair tangling. The Mova V50 scored 93% on sprinkled sand and 70% on ground in. Vacuum wars said it was 88% in their test. But Vacuum wars ranks Ecovacs highly and I had nothing but trouble with my Ecovacs X9 Pro Omni so I don't quite trust them either.
